title: China Tech Revolution Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: US tariffs on Chinese imports escalated to a historic 145% on April 9 after China retaliated with 84% tariffs, prompting a further US hike; China matched at 125%, sending the Hang Seng Tech Index down 4.2% before tech-product exclusion rumors softened the blow. DeepSeek launched a Fast Mode and Expert Mode interface update on April 8, signaling imminent V4 launch on Huawei Ascend 950PR chips expected late April; Spirit AI robotics raised $145M Series B at $1.4B valuation. China formally opposed a US FCC proposal to ban Chinese electronics testing labs, while launching its 21st batch of LEO internet satellites. CAC draft rules on human-like interactive AI services (emotional AI companions) were released for comment, and OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google formed an alliance to combat Chinese AI model distillation.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: DeepSeek implemented a significant update to its chat service on April 8, introducing 'Fast Mode' and 'Expert Mode' as distinct interface tiers alongside its existing standard chat. Chinese robotics startup Spirit AI closed a ¥1 billion ($145 million) Series B round on April 8, co-led by Shunwei Capital and Yunfeng Capital, valuing the company at over ¥10 billion ($1.4 billion).

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Form Alliance to Combat Chinese AI Model Distillation and Cloning. ASML and Tokyo Electron Shares Fall as MATCH Act Advances; Semiconductor Equipment Stocks Rattled. China Launches Feasibility Study for Space-Based Intelligent Computing Constellation.

title: China Tech Revolution Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: DeepSeek's forthcoming V4 frontier model is being built natively on Huawei Ascend chips — the first frontier AI model designed without Nvidia hardware — triggering a surge in Huawei chip orders from ByteDance, Alibaba, and Tencent. Shenzhen activated China's first 10,000-card all-domestic Huawei Ascend AI cluster (14,000 petaflops total, 92% booking rate), directly validating the V4 infrastructure strategy. CXMT recorded a 130% YoY revenue jump to $8B as US export controls accelerated Chinese domestic chip demand; the MATCH Act was introduced in Congress to close allied chip control gaps. Trump signed a 100% pharmaceutical tariff executive order on April 2 targeting China's dominant API supply chain, while China Hang Seng Tech Q4 2025 profits fell 30% — their worst quarterly result in three years.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Industry analysts confirmed on April 5 that Huawei's Ascend 950PR — not the previously-reported 910C — is the specific chip architecture chosen by DeepSeek for its forthcoming V4 frontier model. China's Cyberspace Administration (CAC) released draft Interim Measures on Human-like Interactive AI Services for public comment (deadline May 6, 2026).

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Senator Michael Baumgartner introduced the Multilateral Alignment of Technology Controls on Hardware (MATCH) Act on April 4, co-sponsored by House Select Committee on China Chairman John Moolenaar. Reuters independently confirmed on April 4 the report that DeepSeek's V4 model is being built to run on Huawei's Ascend AI chips.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: DeepSeek V4 Built Natively on Huawei Ascend — First Frontier AI Model Without Nvidia. CNBC reported on April 3 that China's leading chipmakers posted record revenues in 2025, driven by a combination of AI demand and US export controls forcing Chinese companies to source domestically.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: Profits for companies in the 30-member Hang Seng Tech Index fell 30% in Q4 2025 (October–December 2025), the steepest quarterly decline since the 2022 regulatory crackdown. President Trump signed an executive order on April 2 imposing 100% tariffs on patented pharmaceutical products and active pharmaceutical ingredients (API) under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act.

title: China Tech Revolution Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Huawei launched the Ascend 950PR AI inference chip with ByteDance and Alibaba placing immediate orders for ~750,000 units planned through 2026, marking a major step in China's CUDA-compatible domestic AI compute transition. SMIC reported record 2025 revenue of $9.33 billion (+20.9% YoY) with a 2026 action plan targeting above-industry growth — even as unverified US allegations of chip equipment transfers to Iran persisted; China's Foreign Ministry dismissed the claims as 'false information'. DeepSeek suffered its longest outage since launch (13+ hours, March 29-30), exposing infrastructure reliability gaps, while Zhipu AI surged 35% in Hong Kong and ByteDance reportedly plans $14 billion in Nvidia GPU purchases for 2026. China's AI industry crossed 1.2 trillion yuan in core value with 6,200+ enterprises, and the Xiong'an AI Forum unveiled incentives for AI integration into traditional manufacturing sectors.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: Shares of Chinese AI startup Zhipu AI jumped up to 35% in Hong Kong trading on March 31 despite the company reporting a ~60% increase in 2025 net losses. NYC Mayor Mamdani Reverses City Government TikTok Ban. ByteDance is reportedly planning to spend approximately 100 billion yuan (~$14 billion USD) on Nvidia H200 AI GPUs in 2026, according to Tom's Hardware citing industry sources.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: DeepSeek's consumer chatbot platform was fully restored at approximately 10:33 AM CST on March 30, following a two-incident outage spanning more than 13 hours. Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corporation (SMIC) released its 2025 annual results and 2026 action plan. ByteDance and Alibaba Confirm Plans to Order Huawei Ascend 950PR AI Chips.

title: China Tech Revolution Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: US officials alleged that SMIC, China's largest chipmaker, supplied chipmaking equipment and technical training to Iran's military — a potential sanctions violation that could trigger secondary sanctions against China's semiconductor expansion program. DeepSeek granted Huawei exclusive early access to its V4 AI model, deepening the China-domestic AI compute stack and bypassing US hardware restrictions; simultaneously, Tianjin Port achieved 88% automation using a Huawei-DeepSeek AI integration, a flagship showcase for Beijing's 15th Five-Year Plan 'AI+ action plan'. SEMICON China 2026 in Shanghai (March 25–27) projected China's domestic share of 22–40nm chip output to grow from 37% to 42% by 2028. ByteDance's valuation reached $550 billion following the finalized TikTok US divestiture deal in January 2026, in which Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX each acquired 15% stakes.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Reports confirmed that DeepSeek withheld its next-generation V4 model from Nvidia and AMD for optimization, granting early deployment access exclusively to Huawei and Cambricon for inference hardware. Tianjin Port Achieves 88% Automation Using DeepSeek + Huawei PortGPT.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: US Officials Allege SMIC Supplied Chipmaking Technology to Iran's Military. ByteDance's internal valuation reached approximately $550 billion as of late March 2026, according to ChinaPulse, up from $440 billion in prior years.

title: China Tech Revolution Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030), passed at the National People's Congress on March 5, places AI and quantum computing at the heart of national strategy, mentioning AI over 50 times and pledging extraordinary measures to achieve global technological leadership. The plan's 'AI+ action plan' targets integration across every major economic sector, while funding commitments include at least 7% annual R&D growth. In a parallel development, China's cybersecurity regulator CNCERT issued a warning on March 10 about the viral OpenClaw AI agent, flagging data security risks even as major tech firms like Tencent continued to promote it publicly. On March 19, the US DOJ charged Super Micro co-founder Yih-Shyan Liaw and two associates with smuggling $2.5 billion in restricted Nvidia B200 and H200 AI chips to China through Southeast Asian intermediaries, marking the most significant escalation in US export control enforcement since the CHIPS Act era. The case drove Super Micro shares down more than 33% and underscored the growing enforcement gap that Beijing has been exploiting to acquire advanced AI hardware despite US sanctions.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: SEMICON China 2026 opened at the Shanghai New International Expo Centre with 1,500 exhibitors and over 5,000 booths — the largest edition in the event's history.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Fri Mar 20, 2026
summary: One day after his federal indictment, Super Micro co-founder Yih-Shyan Liaw resigned from the company's board of directors.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Thu Mar 19, 2026
summary: US DOJ Charges Super Micro Co-Founder with Smuggling $2.5B in Nvidia AI Chips to China.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Tue Mar 10, 2026
summary: China's national cybersecurity authority CNCERT issued a public warning about OpenClaw, a popular open-source AI agent capable of autonomously controlling devices.

title: China Tech Revolution Tracker Update — Thu Mar 5, 2026
summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030) was unveiled at the fourth session of the 14th National People's Congress.
